feed.title: New Post Quantum Bitcoin Proposal using WInternitz + Lamport auth chains

entry.title: New Post Quantum Bitcoin Proposal using WInternitz + Lamport auth chains
entry.summary:
A cryptographic researcher has recently developed a new post-quantum signature scheme tailored specifically for Bitcoin, utilizing the Winternitz and Lamport methods. This new scheme is designed to be exceptionally lightweight and has already been implemented on 16 different EVM chains. The core of this innovation lies in combining the one-time signature capability of the Winternitz method with a Lamport authorization chain. This combination ensures that each key is used only once, thereby enhancing security.
The researcher's project, which includes detailed proposals for integrating this technology into Bitcoin, will soon become open-source. To facilitate a deeper understanding, a security essay detailing the mechanism of Winternitz + Lamport signing and the overall proposal for Bitcoin can be accessed at [block_opuslux.ar.io](https://block_opuslux.ar.io). Furthermore, the smart contracts associated with this project are set to be verified on-chain within the next 24 hours, ensuring transparency and reliability.
Adjustments have been made to the Winternitz parameter, changing it from 16 to 256, which significantly reduces the size of the signatures, making them more practical for widespread use. This adjustment not only enhances efficiency but also positions the Winternitz signatures as some of the smallest possible quantum-resistant signatures available. The implementation through Taproot facilitates an easier integration process, allowing this new signature scheme to be incorporated seamlessly into existing systems.
The researcher is actively seeking feedback on this innovative approach and invites interested parties to discuss further via email at opusluxofficial@proton.me or through GitHub at opus-lux. This initiative marks a significant step forward in the development of quantum-resistant technologies for Bitcoin, potentially setting a new standard for cryptographic practices in blockchain technology.
